user_message: Pulling in context from Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery's meeting transcript -- need to assess the risk for the executive brief.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-03-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:08:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:13:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:23:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:26:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - executive brief

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-O trap (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-O failure mode (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-O -- Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
